steps.title: Identify the structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
Left side: “In 2020, internationally renowned Slovak photographer Máriá Svarbvá exhibited her work at the Contessa Gallery and the Art Angle Gallery in the United States and Japan, respectively” — an independent clause (subject: Máriá Svarbvá; verb: exhibited).

Right side: “Utilizing vivid pastel colors, overexposed tones, and mirrorlike symmetry, Svarbová's photographs evoke a Socialist-era aesthetic that she describes as “minimalistic but also futuristic.”” — an independent clause (subject: Svarbová's photographs; verb: evoke) introduced by a participial phrase.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: A semicolon separates two related independent clauses. Choice B places the semicolon after “respectively” to join the two independent clauses, with the participial phrase introducing the second clause. Options A and D fail to properly mark the boundary between the clauses, and option C places the semicolon in the wrong location—between “Japan” and “respectively,” where no separation is needed. That matches choice B — B is correct.
